rules: RULE-01: input has level_id or an inline level (grid-row strings). '#' wall, ' ' floor, '.' target, '@' player, '$' box, '*' box-on-target, '+' player-on-target; any other char is a generic tile in state.tiles. Apply input.moves in order up to MAX_MOVES.
RULE-02: A move U/D/L/R steps the player one cell; into floor/target advances the player; log '<dir>:move'.
RULE-03: Into a wall is blocked; log '<dir>:block'.
RULE-04: Into a box: push if the cell beyond is free; log '<dir>:push'; else blocked.
RULE-05: WIN when every box is on a target; set isWon=isGameOver=true and stop. on_target = boxes on targets.
RULE-06: KEY/DOOR (card 1). Door 'D' blocks (player and pushed box) while no key; stepping on 'K' grants the key, removes the 'K', appends '+key' to the log; with the key 'D' behaves like floor.

RULE-07 (THIS CARD): TELEPORT tiles. The level data (including a level with 'T' tiles) is ALREADY in the frozen levels.js — this card edits ONLY move_logic.js. When the player steps onto a tile 'T' (after key pickup), immediately move the player to the matching other 'T' tile in state.tiles, and append '+tp' to that move's log (e.g. 'R:move+tp'). Do NOT chain-teleport from the destination T (apply once per move). Boxes never teleport. Any play on a level with no 'T' tiles produces byte-for-byte identical output to before this card.

files.description: Move resolution. THIS card adds teleport: after a player 'move' onto a 'T' cell (and key pickup), jump the player to the matching other 'T' and append '+tp'. Keep all existing wall/push/move/door/key behavior intact. levels.js is FROZEN.
